creator: clif@eugeneweb.com
text:
Greetings,

This is related to problem reports 179033, and 188897 in that the motherboard is the same. Initially I RMAed the mother board thinking it was a hardware problem. That was before I tested it under Linux. See the attached file of output under various situations and OSes.

As you can see for ifconfig, the "Status:" line does not show up for the cas Nics. Neither does the fourth port cas3. If you run dhclient on cas0 it will fail gracefully as you might expect, if no cable is connected. If a live cable is connected and you run "dhclient cas0" you will eventually get:
cas0: cas_eint: status 0x41000, PCI buss error 0x20
cas0: cas_eint: status 0x41000, PCI buss error 0x20
..
forever with no way to interrupt.

If you merely boot with the cable connected but don't try to run dhclient you will get a few "cas0: device timeout" lines every so often.

However just having the cas quad port card in the system does not prevent re0 from working like the dc card does in PR 188897.

Thanks for a great project and I hope we can clean up these problems with the atom board's slightly nonstandard chipsets.

    Best,
    Clif

***************************************************************************
